content:
I have a web app that was designed and developed to run on a Unix environment, specifically on a kiosk type of setting. It's a run-of-the-mill Maven - Java - Spring project that runs on localhost, with an embedded database that contacts a server platform. Note that it's actually divided into two apps that communicate with each other an then with the server for modularity purposes, but only one has a graphical interface.
It uses some Unix system specific functionalities, but it's modular enough I can just ditch them.
I've ported it to Windows using Launch4J and wrote some batch scripts for installation, used bat2exe and bam, a Windows installer .exe and I have a fully functional PoC.
Now I wanted to the something alike for Android. I may have to redesign everything at some point, but right now I just need to get it functional and in a state I can just come up to a client and say "here install this APK on your phone and see that things are working" or something alike.
What would be my easiest path to do this?

  submitted by   /u/VladTepesDraculea
[link]   [comments]
